Article.ArticleTitle: Fourier Transform Infrared Imaging of Bone.

Abstract.AbstractText: Fourier transform infrared imaging (FTIRI) is a technique that can be used to analyze the material properties of bone using tissue sections. In this chapter, I describe the basic principles of FTIR and the methods for capturing and analyzing FTIR images in bone sections.

University of Angers, Inserm UMR-S 1229 "Regenerative Medicine and Skeleton" & Angers University Hospital, Bone Pathology Unit, Angers, France.
